channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Kuminga brushes off any concerns about mindset after Warriors’ extension talks fall short
item.description: Warriors forward Jonathan Kuminga is set for restricted free agency next summer, but he and GM Mike Dunleavy insist his focus should remain on winning.
item.dc:creator: Michael Nowels, The Mercury News

item.title: Maria Sharapova and the Bryan brothers are elected to the International Tennis Hall of Fame
item.description: Maria Sharapova, a five-time Grand Slam singles champion known for plenty of on-court grit and off-court attention, and Bob and Mike Bryan, twins who won a record 16 major titles in men's doubles together, are first-ballot selections for the International Tennis Hall of Fame.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Parent of WWE and UFC is buying Professional Bull Riders, On Location and IMG for $3.25 billion
item.description: The parent company of WWE and UFC is buying Professional Bull Riders, On Location, and IMG from Endeavor Group in an all-stock deal valued at $3.25 billion.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NBA opens investigation into Joel Embiid's playing status after 76ers star misses season opener
item.description: The NBA is investigating why Philadelphia center Joel Embiid did not play in the team's nationally televised season opener against Milwaukee. The league confirmed the investigation on Thursday. It was opened as part of the NBA's player participation policy that went into effect last season.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: WNBA outlook is promising after record-setting year, although the league has a few challenges ahead
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The WNBA looks promising heading into the offseason after a banner year with record ratings, attendance and a first-time champion in New York.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NY Liberty honored with ticker-tape parade and keys to New York City
item.description: MANHATTAN, N.Y. (PIX11) – The New York Liberty celebrated their first WNBA championship win Thursday with a ticker-tape parade in Manhattan. The parade kicked off at The Battery and made its way along Broadway, also known as the Canyon of Heroes, to City Hall. The parade started at 10 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Washington Mystics part ways with coach Eric Thibault and GM Mike Thibault
item.description: The Washington Mystics are parting ways with coach Eric Thibault and general manager Mike Thibault, the franchise announced Wednesday. Eric Thibault is the fifth WNBA coach to be let go this offseason, joining Dallas, Atlanta, Chicago and Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Fernando Valenzuela changed Los Angeles forever
item.description: The historic impact Fernando Valenzuela made on the city of Los Angeles traces its roots to the 1950s, according to renounced sports commentator Roy Firestone.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Fernando Valenzuela, Mexican-born pitcher whose feats for Dodgers fueled 'Fernandomania,' dies at 63
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fernando Valenzuela, the Mexican-born phenom for the Los Angeles Dodgers who inspired “Fernandomania” while winning the NL Cy Young Award and Rookie of the Year in 1981, has died. He was 63.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Photo Gallery: NL Champs! Dodgers finish Mets to advance to World Series
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers are heading to the 2024 World Series. With a 10-5 home win over the Mets on Sunday, the Dodgers claimed their 25th National League pennant, beating New York 4 games to 2 in the best of seven series.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KTLA 5

item.title: No. 2 Oregon routs Purdue 35-0, remains on top of Big Ten with first road shutout in over 30 years
item.description: WEST LAFAYETTE, Ind. (AP) — Oregon coach Dan Lanning challenged his team to see how it would respond to a long road trip on a short week following its 32-31 victory over then-No. 2 Ohio State last week. The No. 2 Ducks aced that test Friday night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former NFL quarterback Jay Cutler arrested on DUI, gun charges
item.description: The former Bears and Broncos quarterback Jwas arrested Thursday in Tennessee for allegedly driving intoxicated.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, Fox 5

item.title: Former Bears quarterback Jay Cutler arrested for DUI, gun charge
item.description: FRANKLIN, Tenn. — Former Bears quarterback Jay Cutler was arrested Thursday in Tennessee for allegedly driving intoxicated. Officers responded to the 200 block of Bridge Street at around 5 p.m. in Franklin, Tennessee on the report of a minor traffic collision involving two vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, ABC 4
